## ![][image1]

Interviewing Past Clients   
Email & Interview Template

## 

## Email Template 

Hey \[Name\] and \[Name\],

How have you been? \[Personalize your greeting with information about them. This can start with statements like *“I hope you’ve been able to find a time to take that trip to Glacier National Park you talked about planning on your wedding day\!”* or *“It’s been a while since we’ve talked—how are you two and your dog, Bruno?”* or *“I saw on Instagram that you recently bought your first house—congratulations\!”* The more customized and heartfelt, the better\!\]

I’m reaching out because I’m in the process of making some changes to my business to better serve and work with more couples like you. When I think back on my favorite sessions out of everything I’ve ever photographed, the two of you came to mind because \[list out a few of the reasons you loved working with them—i.e. *“Your spontaneity and determination that your wedding day should be the one day you don’t have to conform to anyone else’s expectations.”* Be genuine\!\]. 

In the future, I would love to work with more \[a positive adjective you would use to describe your ideal client avatars—i.e. “*adventurous*” or “*family-focused*.” You can look back to the “type of couple” section in “Start to Brainstorm Your Specialty” and “Similarities from List \#2: Describe Your Favorite Clients That Made You Feel the MOST Valued” exercises in the fillable template for some ideas.\] and \[add another secondary positive adjective here—i.e. *“unique”* or *“enthusiastic”*\] couples like you, and I thought the best way to learn how to do that would be by talking to you directly and getting more insight into:

* why you chose to have the beautiful \[wedding/elopement\] day you did  
* how you found me and what made you choose me to be your photographer  
* what goals or worries/anxieties you had leading up to your \[wedding/elopement\] day that might have influenced your decisions   
* what stood out to you the most about working with me 

**Would you have 15-20 minutes anytime within the next 2 weeks for me to call you** and ask you some questions to help me work with more couples who share your \[mention something important to them—whether that’s the type of wedding day they had (*“plan to have a friends-only elopement day”)*, their values (*“your determination to have a zero waste wedding day”)*, or an issue they overcame *(“struggle to find accessible mountaintop ceremony spots”)*. This will be more effective if you can speak to a moral or philosophical problem they genuinely care about, even beyond their wedding day\!\]?

It would be so helpful to learn more about the process from your perspective, and I truly value your honesty and insight. \[This next sentence is optional but recommended:\] To show my appreciation, I would like to offer you a \[small gift, like *“$15 gift card to your favorite coffee shop”* or *“a 8x10” print of a photo from your wedding gallery of your choosing, shipped directly to you”*\] to thank you for your time\!

If you’re interested, \[details about how to best schedule a call with you, i.e. *“you can schedule a call for whenever works best for you using my Calendly”* or *“I’m available to chat this week anytime on Wednesday or Friday after 2pm. When works best for you?”*\] 

 \[Give them a final compliment: *“I had the best time getting to know you on your wedding day, and I am excited to have the chance to catch up and chat\!”*\] 

Thanks so much—I hope to hear from you soon\!

 \[Sign off\]

## Suggested questions for a phone/video interview

*Pro tip: read through all of these out loud & get confidence asking them before you talk to your past clients. Omit any that are not relevant or feel repetitive to you.*

**Understanding their planning journey**

* When did you two first start thinking about getting married? How long had you been together? Was there an official proposal and/or engagement ring(s)? What did those initial conversations of “how should we get married” like?   
* Did you always know you were going to have the \[wedding/elopement\] you ended up having? Or how did your decision process unfold?  
* What was most important to each of you when it came to your decisions on when/where/and how you were going to get married? What were each of your “top priorities” (i.e. was your top priority a particular location, scenery, season, date/month, size of wedding, type of venue/style/vibe, activities, aesthetics, experience?) How did you start narrowing down what you wanted to do & why?  
* Do you remember what were your original “must haves” or “non-negotiables” when it came to:  
  * WHEN your wedding/elopement was going to be  
  * WHERE your wedding/elopement was going to be  
  * WHO you would invite or not invite  
  * WHAT you were going to do or experience or what it would look like  
  * HOW you were going to plan it or pay for it or do/not do any of the legal process  
* Where did you go to get ideas, inspiration, and information to help you figure these things out? (I.e. Do you remember anything you googled? Did you look on Instagram? How did you use it? Did you use Pinterest? Can I see what you saved on IG or what’s on your original Pinterest board if you still have it? Do you remember any websites or apps you used? Did you join any planning facebook groups? Did you ask friends/family for ideas/help/advice?  
* Do you remember any particular “ah ha” moments in your planning process that really helped you nail down a more clear idea of what you wanted to do for your wedding day?  
* Did you get engagement photos taken or think about doing an engagement session? What was the main purpose of getting engagement photos taken (if you did)?  
* Did you have conversations with your family or friends about how you wanted to get married? How did those go? Were there any challenges or obstacles that you had to overcome regarding your family/friends opinions or desires in when, where, or how you got married?   
* Thinking back on your planning process before you hired me, what were the biggest challenges, frustrations, or fears you remember having?

**Understanding why they chose you**

* Do you remember when you first started thinking about needing to search for a photographer for your wedding/elopement? When in your planning process was that?  
* Did you already know about my business at that point? Or were did you start doing your initial searches for a wedding/elopement photographer? (I.e. Do you remember anything you googled? Did you look on Instagram? How did you use it? Did you use Pinterest? Do you remember any websites or apps you used? Did you join any planning facebook groups? Did you ask friends/family for ideas/help/advice?)  
* What were your biggest priorities in your mind when it came to hiring a wedding/elopement photographer? What were you looking for?   
* Had you ever hired a professional photographer before? What was your biggest challenge or fear about hiring a photographer for your wedding?  
* In as much detail as possible, how did you first find out about my business and what were your first impressions of it?   
* Were you considering any other photographers in addition to me? Did you inquire with them or just look at their websites? What was it about me & my business that stood out to you?  
* What parts of my marketing did you encounter and influence your decision in hiring me? How much of my website did you look at? Did you look at any of my social media profiles?   
* If you had to prioritize 1-2 main reasons why you choose me over other photographers, what would it be? (Photography style, something I offer in my packages, something about who I am as a person like my personality, beliefs, values, etc. or my experience/expertise, where I’m located, my price, or something else). What “pushed you over the edge” to hire me or sealed the deal for you?  
* What do you think sets me apart from other photographers? How do you think I am different?


**Understanding the value they received from you (the transformation you helped them achieve)**

* What was the most helpful aspect of working with me before your \[wedding/elopement\]? Were there any unexpected benefits?  
* What was your favorite thing about your experience with me on the day of your \[wedding/elopement\]? Was there anything that surprised you that you really loved?  
* What was your favorite thing about the images you received? Was there anything surprising about them?  
* Is there anything else you really liked or appreciated about working with me that you think couples considering hiring me should know?  
* Having had this experience with me and having received your images, if you got to have your \[wedding/elopement\] again, would you hire me again? What would be your main reason you’d want to hire me again?  
* If you wanted to hire me again, how much more could I charge and you’d still hire me? (Honestly?)  
* Is there anything you think I could improve on in my workflow, communication, or anything else that would help future couples who work with me?  
* Do you have any ideas or advice for me on how you think I could better market myself or get more couples just like you two to hire me?

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
